methods.name: Peer-Median

data.narrative:
SpliceBio is a Series B-stage biotech company that has raised $135M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 8 private companies and 3 public comparables in the Biotech & Health sector, though the public comp methodology could not be applied due to absence of revenue disclosure.

Two triangulation methods yielded actionable results. Stage-progression analysis, applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implied a valuation of $256M. Peer-median anchoring across five same-stage, same-sector private comparables (Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, New Limit, Asimov, and Profluent) produced a median of $810M, with a range spanning $600M to $3.3B. The dispersion reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity and commercial trajectory within the peer set.

Triangulating these two methods yields a valuation range of $218M (low) to $931M (high), with a median of $533M. The wide spread reflects tension between the conservative stage-progression anchor and the elevated peer median, likely driven by the outsized valuations of lead peers Eikon ($3.3B) and Neko Health ($1.8B). Key uncertainties include SpliceBio's specific IP position relative to the peer set and whether its capital efficiency and technical progress justify positioning toward the upper or lower end of the range. Revenue trajectory and upcoming catalyst timing would meaningfully reduce this band.
